findings.description: AdminAwardsPage is importing named exports (SelectContent, SelectItem, etc.) from @/components/ui/select, but the file only contains a default export of a custom Select component, leading to a build/runtime failure.
findings.fix_suggestion: Restore the standard shadcn/ui Select component or update AdminAwardsPage to use the custom Select component correctly, ensuring all required sub-components are available.

findings.description: AdminAwardsPage and many of its sub-components use English labels (e.g., 'Awards Management', 'Name', 'Category', 'Actions') which is inconsistent with the project's requirement for Chinese labels.
findings.fix_suggestion: Translate all UI text and labels to Chinese to match the rest of the application.

findings.description: SessionPage, VotesPage, and UsersPage use useSearchParams() but are not wrapped in a Suspense boundary, which can cause hydration errors or client-side rendering issues in Next.js static builds.
findings.fix_suggestion: Wrap the content of these pages in a Suspense boundary, similar to how SchoolsPage is implemented.

findings.description: DataTable has hardcoded English strings like 'Search...', 'No results.', and 'Rows per page', which violates the requirement for Chinese label consistency.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace hardcoded English strings with Chinese translations or make them configurable via props.

findings.description: SchoolsPage uses native alert() for error handling, whereas other pages like SessionPage use the custom Alert component, leading to an inconsistent user experience.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace native alert() and confirm() calls with UI components like Alert or Dialog for a consistent and professional look.

findings.description: KVEditor's updateKey logic updates the object key on every keystroke, which is buggy: it can cause data loss if the user types a key that already exists and creates churn in the state.
findings.fix_suggestion: Refactor key editing to use local state and only trigger onChange when the user finishes editing (e.g., on blur or enter), and add validation to prevent duplicate keys.

findings.description: The custom Select component contains hardcoded Chinese text '选择奖项' in its Head sub-component, making it less reusable for non-award-related selections.
findings.fix_suggestion: Make the header text a prop or remove it to make the Select component more generic.

findings.description: AdminAwardsPage uses local state for page and pageSize instead of syncing them with the URL via searchParams, which is inconsistent with SessionPage and SchoolsPage.
findings.fix_suggestion: Update AdminAwardsPage to use URL-based state for pagination to support deep linking and consistent navigation behavior.

passed_checks: Role-based access control is implemented in AdminLayout and verified on individual pages.
Data-table component is consistently used across most admin views for structured data display.
Form validation (basic) is present in session and user management dialogs.
Responsive design considerations are visible in layout and some pages (flex-col on mobile).
Tailwind CSS is used consistently for styling.
summary: The Admin UI implementation provides a solid foundation but suffers from significant inconsistencies and a critical integration error. Specifically, the replacement of the standard Select component broke AdminAwardsPage, and there is a lack of Chinese label consistency across several files. Additionally, the use of useSearchParams without Suspense and inconsistent state management for pagination should be addressed for better UX and reliability.
